title: API Keys And Personal Access Tokens Semantic Contract
description: Source-backed draft semantic contract for the api keys personal access tokens capability, derived from historical source-lineage evidence as lineage. Facets cite verified source-import manifest rows; proof is hermetic/runtime lineage and not fresh USF proof.
aiGuidance: Source-backed draft. Facets cite verified manifest source rows as lineage; proof is hermetic/stale and not current readiness. Do not treat as implementation authority or fresh proof; respect the hexagonal constraint in ADR 0005.

lifecycle.description: API key lifecycle is explicit: creation returns a plaintext secret exactly once, listing exposes metadata only, authentication validates a live non-revoked secret, and revocation sets a revoked timestamp after which authentication returns no actor. The plaintext secret is never persisted or re-shown.

stateModel.description: State is active versus revoked, with creation and last-used timestamps, an optional expiry, a revoked timestamp, a key prefix, scopes, and hash/salt storage fields. The plaintext secret is explicitly not part of persisted state.

permissions.description: Tenant operations require tenant API-key read/write permissions and operator read requires the platform API-key read permission, and creation additionally requires an API-access entitlement that is deny-by-default. Tenant isolation prevents one tenant from listing another tenant's keys.

contracts.description: The operation surface lists, creates, and deletes tenant keys, reads developer context, and provides an operator read of a given tenant's keys. Creation returns the one-time secret; all other reads return metadata only.

validation.description: Creation is rejected for tenants without the API-access entitlement, invalid key identifiers are rejected at the route, and garbage, wrong, hash-presented, or expired secrets never authenticate. Cross-tenant listing is prevented.

errorModel.description: Usecase results include a not-entitled and an ok variant, invalid route identifiers return 400, and revoked or malformed secrets authenticate as no actor; provider failure returns a typed degraded result fail-closed. Authentication failures never leak whether a key existed.

auditModel.description: Creation and revocation emit key-created and key-revoked audit-before-change events, and a failing audit aborts creation before the key is stored. Metadata reads are not audited as mutations.

readinessModel.description: Readiness is always-on once the data substrate is migrated, and degraded mode disables store-dependent mutation while keeping read-only readiness and audit visible. Live proofs skip honestly when the substrate is unavailable and never fake-pass.

proof.description: A unit proof covers key lifecycle and authentication, and runtime proofs validate the live repository, crypto, and row-level isolation plus live route-handler behaviour including entitlement gating, demonstrated in source lineage via hermetic/runtime validation. Proof is hermetic/runtime lineage; fresh USF proof pending USF-101/USF-59.

uiSemanticDefinition.description: No verified UI source path in the manifest yet; author the UI semantic model or import the UI source row before generation.
